transcript: Y'all, brand new rumors about the rides at Disney's Villains Land are swirling around. And even if half of what's being whispered about ends up true, it's going to be wicked. Meanwhile, Disney announced a new attraction for Hollywood Studios, and it's already stirring up some controversy. Over on the West Coast, we finally have the full menus for Disney California Adventure Food and Wine Festival. And yes, there are some wild cards we did not see coming. And the DFB team is packing their bags because we are about to head out on a huge adventure you won't want to miss. All the latest Disney and theme park news is coming up right now here on DFB Guide. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food and I got a whole lot of news to tell you about today. So buckle up. Here we go. New details about Villains Land at Magic Kingdom are making the rounds. According to a new report from The RAP, citing sources familiar with the project. Some big changes could be in the works. Here's what's being rumored. And the key word here is rumored. An Emperor's New Groove family coaster. Now, originally, concept art showed a massive Maleficent thrill coaster. Well, it looked kind of like a Maleficent situation with Briers and stuff, but according to sources, that idea may have evolved into a more familyfriendly coaster like Slinky Dog Dash, themed to the Emperor's New Groove, and allegedly there is a pull the lever moment. But Maleficent would still have a major presence in the land, according to these reports. Maleficent may anchor a large scale indoor water-based attraction reportedly ambitious enough to rival rides like Rise of the Resistance or Radiator Springs Racers. If you've seen the Pirates of the Caribbean ride in Shanghai, that's the vibe we're talking about. A Hades dinner show might be on its way. Hades with Pain and Panic could headline a fullervice prefix dinner show experience, replacing an earlier Madame Medusa concept. And also, a third unannounced ride. Sources say a smaller flat ride may also be planned, possibly an Ursula themed spinner attraction like Dumbo. Updated plans are reportedly due to Disney leadership soon with board approval potentially following and many expect we could hear more at D23 this year in Anaheim. Again, all speculation you guys sources familiar with the project. What does that even mean? Nothing has been officially announced by Disney. Still, if even some of this is in the works, that could be very, very cool. Big news for Hollywood Studios. We've got brand new details about the Magic of Disney animation experience. And it's a little bit controversial. Disney revealed Olaf Draws, a brand new animation academy style class coming to the re-imagined Magic of Disney animation area in Hollywood Studios in late summer 2026. transcript: When you're in Disney World, there is no escape from the continuous eyes of the mouse. But maybe it's better that way. Here's a behind-the-scenes look into how Disney World security is able to keep tabs on you all vacation long and why that's crucial information for your upcoming trip. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. Thousands upon thousands of guests enter the Disney World parks every single day. And yet, Disney still has their sights set on you. Yep, you. But I'm not here to just tell you that. I'm here to prove it. While also assuring you that you're going to be 110% grateful for the security spotlight when all is said and done. Outside of every single Disney World park, before you reach the front gate, Tap Styles, you'll encounter a security checkpoint. This checkpoint is made up of a dozen odd security cast members as well as a few walkthrough security detectors like you'd find at the airport. But thankfully, you don't have to take off your shoes for this one. Instead, you will probably benefit from taking things out of your park bag that might set security detectors off, like any large electronics like portable chargers and tablets, metal items including mint tins, cases for your sunglasses or glasses, umbrellas, or really just anything that you're questioning if it'll trigger security or not. I mean, if you got the hands to hold those items and walk them through the security checkpoint out of your bag, you might as well. So, what happens then if you accidentally leave one of these electronics or metal items in your bag, and it does trigger security? Well, you're not in trouble or anything. Owning an umbrella is not a federal offense yet, but you will be directed to enter a second line where you'll go through a more thorough bag check before you're clear to go. During this more thorough bag check, if a security member finds something in your bag you're not allowed to have, like glass containers, or outside alcohol, drug paraphernalia, selfie sticks, or something else that's banned from the parks, you're not going to get wheeled off to the brrig or anything, unless it's really bad, of course. But you will be asked to either dispose of the banned item or backtrack to your vehicle or hotel room to put it away before you're allowed to start your day. And that's a major time suck. So, just don't bring banned items. Maybe we have a whole video about what's banned, so you can just watch that and make sure you don't have that stuff. Now, if you're bringing a stroller or a wheelchair with you to the parks that day, those will not go through the same security detectors. Instead, you'll have to take them through a different line, which is usually just directly to the left or right of the regular security checkpoint, and you'll have it more thoroughly inspected by a security cast member. Kids in the strollers will still be requir
notes: General security overview — no new policy changes, just educational content about bag checks and surveillance.

transcript: Do you want to go on a Disney World vacation, but you're already exhausted thinking about all the lines and the crowds and the lack of personal space? We're here to show you how to survive Disney World, even when it's packed. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. There are three things that I really have to brace myself for every time I go to Disney World. Price hikes, heat exhaustion, and crowds. We're talking so many people everywhere. Even during their slow seasons, Disney World still has thousands upon thousands of guests swarming their parks each day. But if navigating those crowds is the sole reason why you haven't locked in your big Disney World vacation yet, let me show you how to juggle the parks, the hotels, and Disney Springs in ways that'll even make the busiest day more manageable and more fun. So, Epcot has two separate entrances. The more popular and more crowded entrance tends to be at the front of the park, which will put you right where Spaceship Earth is, and the less popular and therefore less crowded entrance is at the back of the park, Epcot's International Gateway, which is located right between the France pavilion and the UK pavilion. The World Showcase is always less crowded before everything officially opens up for the day since most pavilion offerings won't open till 11:00 a.m. However, the rides in all of those World Showcase Pavilions open right as early theme park entry starts. So, if you use the International Gateway, you'll be right there next to Remy's Ratatouille Adventure just as soon as you enter into Epcot, giving you a straight shot at being one of the first folks in line for it at the start of the day. Then you can go over to Lelay all and have a nice relaxing breakfast and then wander through the World Showcase a little bit before all the crowds make their way over there. Waiting in long lines for characters is not the most fun. Waiting no time for the characters with way fewer people surrounding you, super fun. Now, in the past, being able to see Disney characters pop up around the hotels was a nice hidden treat, but nothing was ever guaranteed. But lately, some of the Disney resorts have scheduled character appearances, so you don't have to gamble on whether or not you'll be visiting a resort when one of these free meet and greets is taking place. Normally, scheduled character appearances take place at Porteran's French Quarter and Wilderness Lodge. But during Cool Kids Summer, taking place from May 26th to September 8th, you'll also be able to find scheduled character meet and greets at additional hotels, including Art of Animation, Caribbean Beach, Pop Century, and Porter's Riverside. Okay, this one's weird, I know, but in our personal experience, and as you know, we've got reporters in the parks all day, every day, Disney resorts that have convention centers do tend to have fairly peaceful locations with little to no guests
notes: Crowd tips — EPCOT gateway strategy already in our data, Cool Kids Summer is May-Sep only (not Jan), no new actionable info.
